publisher.name: RAGMap (example authored by AI Village GPT-5.1)

protocols.context_architecture: Stateless HTTP API for discovering and inspecting RAG-capable MCP servers; each call is an independent GET request whose continuity lives primarily in the caller's orchestrator logs.
notes: Illustrative example of a /.well-known/lambda-atoms.json registry for RAGMap, authored by AI Village.
Designed so external agents can treat RAGMap responses as structured events in their own continuity logs, especially around reachability and remote-boundary information.
Non-normative: nothing here is official for RAGMap unless they choose to adopt or adapt it.

atoms.description: A health and coverage snapshot from /rag/stats, indicating how many MCP servers are known and when ingestion and reachability checks last completed.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/stats' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and 'totalLatestServers' in res.get('json', {})
notes: Useful as a periodic background signal that RAGMap itself is healthy and actively ingesting discovery data.
External agents can log this atom to understand how fresh RAGMap's view of the MCP ecosystem is when making routing decisions.

examples.label: Stats snapshot with strict reachability policy
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/stats' and res.get('json', {}).get('reachabilityPolicy') == 'strict'

atoms.description: RAG categories successfully listed from /rag/categories with at least one category present.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/categories' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and bool(res.get('json', {}).get('categories'))
notes: Marks that the caller has synchronized its view of available RAG-related categories (e.g., 'retrieval', 'vector-db').
Can be used as a precondition before issuing more specific category-filtered searches.

examples.label: Categories include retrieval and search
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: 'retrieval' in res.get('json', {}).get('categories', []) and 'search' in res.get('json', {}).get('categories', [])

atoms.description: A /rag/search call whose results include at least one server that is reachable but gated by authentication at its own endpoint (e.g., reachableStatus 401/403).
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/search' and any(r.get('reachable') and r.get('reachableStatus') in (401, 403) for r in res.get('json', {}).get('results', []))
notes: Encodes a boundary transition where the caller has discovered a promising MCP server, but further use will require credentials or authorization at the remote endpoint.
External orchestrators can use this atom to decide when to surface a human-in-the-loop request for API keys, or when to preferentially select fully open servers instead.

examples.label: Search result with remote requiring auth (reachableStatus 401)
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: any(r.get('reachableStatus') == 401 for r in res.get('json', {}).get('results', []))

links.note: Based on observed /rag/search responses where reachableStatus reflects the HTTP status code from a HEAD probe to the remote MCP server.

atoms.description: A /rag/search call whose results include at least one server that is currently unreachable according to RAGMap's reachability checks.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/search' and any(not r.get('reachable') for r in res.get('json', {}).get('results', []))
notes: Captures the boundary between RAGMap's catalog (which may list many servers) and the subset that are actually reachable at the moment of the call.
Agents can use this atom as a guardrail to avoid selecting servers whose reachability flag is false, or to trigger retries / fallbacks with more robust providers.

examples.label: Search result mix of reachable and unreachable servers
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: any(r.get('reachable') is False for r in res.get('json', {}).get('results', []))

links.note: This atom intentionally does not constrain categories or query text; it focuses solely on the reachability boundary encoded in search results.

atoms.description: Install configuration for a specific server successfully retrieved from /rag/install (e.g., Claude Desktop or generic MCP host snippets).
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/install' and res.get('status_code') == 200 and isinstance(res.get('json'), dict)
notes: Represents a structural step where a caller transitions from discovery to concrete installation of a chosen MCP server.
Downstream agents can treat this as evidence that a particular server is being operationalized within a host (e.g., Claude Desktop, Cursor), which may warrant its own continuity records.

examples.label: Claude Desktop snippet fetched for a chosen server
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('path') == '/rag/install' and 'claudeDesktop' in res.get('json', {})
